briefdescription.para: These constants specify different types of handlers and is used to differentiate interrupt requests from the XI2s Receiver peripheral.
These constants specify different channel ID's.

sectiondef.header: AES Status and Register Masks and Shifts

briefdescription.para: Use of Channel Status Block bit shift.
Use of auxiliary sample bits bit shift.
Indication of Alignment level bit shift.
Sampling Frequency scaling flag bit shift.
Alphanumeric channel origin data register(s) byte0 mask.
Alphanumeric channel origin data register(s) byte1 mask.
Alphanumeric channel origin data register(s) byte1 shift.
Alphanumeric channel origin data register(s) byte2 mask.
Alphanumeric channel origin data register(s) byte2 shift.
Alphanumeric channel origin data register(s) byte3 mask.
Alphanumeric channel origin data register(s) byte3 shift.
Alphanumeric channel destination data register(s) byte0 mask.
Alphanumeric channel destination data register(s) byte1 mask.
Alphanumeric channel destination data register(s) byte1 shift.
Alphanumeric channel destination data register(s) byte2 mask.
Alphanumeric channel destination data register(s) byte2 shift.
Alphanumeric channel destination data register(s) byte3 mask.
Alphanumeric channel destination data register(s) byte3 shift.
Local sample address code register(s) byte0 mask.
Local sample address code register(s) byte1 mask.
Local sample address code register(s) byte1 shift.
Local sample address code register(s) byte2 mask.
Local sample address code register(s) byte2 shift.
Local sample address code register(s) byte3 mask.
Local sample address code register(s) byte3 shift.
Time-of-day sample address code register(s) byte0 mask.
Time-of-day sample address code register(s) byte1 mask.
Time-of-day sample address code register(s) byte1 shift.
Time-of-day sample address code register(s) byte2 mask.
Time-of-day sample address code register(s) byte2 shift.
Time-of-day sample address code register(s) byte3 mask.
Time-of-day sample address code register(s) byte3 shift.
These constants specify different types of handlers and is used to differentiate interrupt requests from the XI2s Receiver peripheral.

description.para: Register offsets for the XI2srx device.

sectiondef.header: Core Configuration Register masks and shifts

briefdescription.para: Maximum number of channels bit shift.

sectiondef.header: Core Control Register masks and shifts

briefdescription.para: Latch AES Channel Status bit shift.
AES Block Complete Interrupt bit shift.
Audio Overflow Detected Interrupt bit shift.

sectiondef.header: XI2S Timing Control Register masks and shifts
Audio Channel Control Register masks and shifts

briefdescription.para: This macro reads a value from a XI2s Receiver register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is read from the register. The most significant data will be read as 0.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XI2s Receiver core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file).

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XI2s_Rx_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: This macro writes a value to a XI2s Receiver register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XI2s Receiver core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file) to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XI2s_Rx_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: This typedef specifies the input sources of the the XI2s Receiver.
This typedef specifies the justification of the the XI2s Receiver.
Callback function pointer type for I2S RX events.

detaileddescription.para: This typedef defines the signature for callback functions that handle I2S receiver (RX) events. The callback is invoked when specific I2S RX events occur during operation.

parameterdescription.para: A void pointer to user-defined callback reference data. This pointer is typically used to pass context or state information to the callback handler.

simplesect.para: The actual callback implementation should cast CallbackRef to the appropriate data type as needed for application-specific processing.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the XI2s Receiver.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the core. Initialization of the XI2s Receiver includes setting up the instance data, and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s Receiver instance.
points to the configuration structure associated with the XI2s Receiver.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: This function enables/disables the XI2s Receiver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s Receiver instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the XI2s Receiver.

briefdescription.para: This function requests the XI2s Receiver to latch the AES Channel Status bits from the registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s Receiver instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified interrupt of the XI2s Receiver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s Receiver instance.
is a bit mask of the interrupts to be enabled.

simplesect.para: XI2srx_HW for the available interrupt masks.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified interrupt of the XI2s Receiver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s Receiver instance.
is a bit mask of the interrupts to be disabled.

simplesect.para: XI2s_Receiver_HW for the available interrupt masks.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the input source for the specified AXI-Stream channel pair.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s Receiver instance.

specifies the AXI-Stream channel pair
0 : AXI-Stream channel 0 and 1
1 : AXI-Stream channel 2 and 3
2 : AXI-Stream channel 4 and 5
3 : AXI-Stream channel 6 and 7
specifies the input source

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE : if the AXI-Stream channel pair is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the SCLK Output divider value of the I2s timing generator.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the I2s Receiver instance.
is the frequency of the MClk.

is the sampling frequency of the system. Divider value for the SCLK generation MCLK/SCLK = SCLKOUT_DIV x 2 i.e. MCLK = 384xFs, SCLK = 48xFs (2x24bits) -> SCLKOUT_DIV = MCLK/SCLK/2 = 4 Valid values are 1 through 15.

simplesect.para:
- XST_FAILURE if SCLK Output divider is not calculated to be a positive integer.
XST_SUCCESS, otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the AES Channel Status bits to insert.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s Receiver instance.
is a pointer to a buffer containing the AES channel status bits.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the captured AES Channel Status bits.

detaileddescription.para: This will clear all the 6 channel status registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx core instance.
is a pointer to the XI2s Receiver instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or disable the justification.

briefdescription.para: This function is to enable right/left justification.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s Receiver instance.

is a enum to select the left or right justification.
XI2S_RX_JUSTIFY_LEFT : Left justification
XI2S_RX_JUSTIFY_RIGHT : Right justification

briefdescription.para: This inline function reads the XI2s Receiver version.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This inline function clears the specified interrupt of the XI2s Receiver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx core instance.
is a bit mask of the interrupts to be cleared.

simplesect.para: XI2srx_HW for the available interrupt masks.

briefdescription.para: This inline function enables the XI2s Receiver logging.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This inline function disables the XI2s Receiver logging.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: The self-test reads the version register, data width , max channels, Master or slave configuration and verifies the values

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful i.e. if the self test passes.
XST_FAILURE if unsuccessful i.e. if the self test fails

briefdescription.para: This function returns a reference to an XI2srx_Config structure based on the core id, DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xi2srx_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique core ID of the XI2s Receiver core for the lookup operation.

simplesect.para: returns a reference to a config record in the configuration table corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific XI2s_Rx instance such that the driver is ready to use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx instance to be worked on.

is the unique id of the device controlled by this XI2s_Rx instance. Passing in a device id associates the generic XI2s_Rx instance to a specific device, as chosen by the caller or application developer.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the device was not found in the configuration such that initialization could not be accomplished.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the XI2s Receiver driver.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from the XI2s Receiver peripheral, determines the source of the interrupts, clears the interrupts and calls callbacks accordingly.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx instance.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx core instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is a pointer to the callback function.
is a reference pointer passed on actual calling of the callback function.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the contents of the logging buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx instance.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the contents of the logging buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx instance.

briefdescription.para: This function writes XI2s Receiver logs into the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx instance.
is the log event type.
is the log data.

simplesect.para: Log write is done only if the log is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the next item in the logging buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx instance.

simplesect.para: When the buffer is filled, the next log item is returned. When the buffer is empty, NULL is returned.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the source buffer and writes to a destination buffer.

detaileddescription.para:
Before calling this API user application should write 192 bits i.e. 24 bytes to the array I2srx_SrcBuf.
I2srx_SrcBuf
is the source buffer which has 192 channel status bits.
I2srx_DstBuf
is the destination buffer to store the 24 bytes.

briefdescription.para: This inline function returns the XI2s data width.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: The XI2s data width in number of bits

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the maximum number of XI2s channels available.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the XI2s operating mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI2s_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This file implements the channel status format related functions.

detaileddescription.para: Contains an basic self-test API that displays the version of the I2S Receiver device.
Format status related offsets & masks definitions related to the channel status format.For formats/line protocols check the AES standard specifications document.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release. 1.1 kar 04/02/18 Changed Channel Status clear API to clear all regs. 2.0 kar 09/28/18 Added new API to enable justification. Added new API to select left/right justification. 2.1 pg 01/30/20 Added 32bit_LR support is added to calculate i2s clock for 32bit mode.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release. 1.1 kar 04/02/18 Added debug log function prototypes. 2.0 kar 09/28/18 Added new API to enable justification. Added new API to select left/right justification. 2.1 pg 01/30/20 Added 32bit_LR support is added to calculate i2s clock for 32bit mode. Added variable Is32bitLR to XI2srx_Config structure and updated xi2srx.c file

detaileddescription.para: For formats/line protocols check the AES standard specifications document. MODIFICATION HISTORY:

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release.

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release.

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release. 1.1 kar 04/02/18 Changed log APIs to take i2srx instance as argument.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release. 1.1 kar 04/02/18 Moved debug Function prototypes to xi2srx.h file

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release. 2.0 kar 09/28/18 Added justification enable masks and shifts. Added left and right justification masks and shifts.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release. 1.1 kar 04/02/18 Changed log write API's argument to i2srx instance.

detaileddescription.para: Additionally, it also verifies and displays the configuration of the drivers for i2s data width, if the Receiver core IsMaster, and the maximum channels supported by the I2s device.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kar 01/25/18 Initial release.
